title: S-Prove proposition-proof contract
description: Package-level extension for proving displayed propositions and invariant-register entries in a structural-exhaustion proof state. S-Prove extends the framework lemma-schema alphabet to operationalize displayed propositions from H_0, E, I, R, V, Q, and A as specified in the structural exhaustion framework (branch_closure_methodology_extended.tex, proof language and runtime model).

schema.description: Required contract discriminator; it must be the literal `S-Prove`.

proposition.description: Required mathematical statement to be proved as a displayed proposition, invariant-register entry, exclusion, residual fact, payload fact, or certificate premise in the current branch state B.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose formulation of the proposition, with all quantified objects, hypotheses, constants, asymptotic qualifications, and conclusions needed to apply it.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX rendering of the same proposition, using the notation recorded in the proof language L and vocabulary record V.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the proof assistant or formal language in which `formal` is encoded.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable statement logically equivalent to the informal and LaTeX formulations.

assumptions.description: Required list of assumptions actually used by the proof; use an empty list only for an unconditional statement. Entries must name the supplying branch-state components such as H_0, E, I, R, V, Q, A, ledger deposits, slack table, defect register, or resilience register.

items.description: One mathematical assumption on which the proposition depends.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose statement of the assumption, including its scope and quantified objects.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX rendering of the assumption in the notation declared by V or the proof language L.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used for this assumption.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable encoding of the same assumption.

method.description: Optional concise identification of the proof method, such as direct target test (CT1), minimality/replacement (CT2), charging or budget accounting (CT4/CT5/CT13), finite verified exhaustion (CT8/CT10/C5), routing through P_{i->j}, or aggregate/rank closure (CT14/CT15).

evidence.description: Required nonempty list of verifiable sources that discharge the proposition; references should resolve to framework labels, contract instances, dependency-table steps, worksheet rows, finite tables, audited code hashes, or formal artifacts.

items.description: One evidence record supporting the proposition.

kind.description: Required category of evidence, chosen from the schema's fixed evidence-kind alphabet; select the category that describes how the cited source establishes the proposition.

reference.description: Required nonempty locator for the evidence, preferably a stable framework label, contract-instance id, worksheet row, branch-table row, verification theorem, or audited artifact.

hash.description: Optional integrity hash of an external code, table, proof-assistant artifact, or other version-sensitive evidence object.

notes.description: Optional explanation of which part of the cited evidence proves the proposition and of any asymptotic or computational qualification.
